title: Master Form - Consolidating All Forms
excerpt: I have a board with several form views. I want users to be able to go to one form URL. From there, the user will see one drop down box with a list of the various forms to choose from. They would choose the form and it wo…

posts.author: jmifsud (Joseph Mifsud)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a board with several form views. I want users to be able to go to one form URL. From there, the user will see one drop down box with a list of the various forms to choose from. They would choose the form and it would populate below. Is this possible through Monday or a marketplace app? Or is there a similar solution that anyone can think of?

Thank you!

posts.author: GCavin (Gilles Cavin - Reinventing Formulas in monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Joseph,

Have you thought of creating a monday document with all the links?

Want to take your formulas to the next level? Try the Advanced Formula Booster, the app that reinvents formulas in monday.

  • Create formulas without using the Formula column (and avoid its limitations)
  • Build formulas involving data from the previous item, the next item, the sub-items, the parent item, even items in the same group or the same board.
  • In one formula, update multiple columns from multiple items.

Check our blog for real use cases.

posts.author: jmifsud (Joseph Mifsud)
posts.cookedHtml: That is what I am doing now. A single document listing all the forms and links. Most of the users are mobile,… so I thought if I could have their browser default to a landing form, they could just go from there.

posts.author: GCavin (Gilles Cavin - Reinventing Formulas in monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml: Would be an easy job for any web developer. Do you have a web site?

posts.author: jmifsud (Joseph Mifsud)
posts.cookedHtml: We do. I’ll see if this is something my guy can do. Thank you!
